justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly identified with detailed explanation of mixed read sources across instances. (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 explicitly called out in contract_compliance and escalations. (3) Non-resumable backfill identified as a risk with specific failure scenario. (4) All three specific recommendations present — validation query/row-count comparison, feature flag for atomic read-source switch, cursor-based resumable backfill with checkpointing. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — identified all three required gaps plus additional risks (dual-write atomicity, backfill race with concurrent writes, rollback danger after Step 5)
Recommendations are concrete and actionable: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for backfill race, feature flag for atomic cutover, checkpoint-based resumability
Escalations correctly routed to MJ with clear blast-radius reasoning
Observability review adds significant operational value beyond what the rubric requires — debugging capability assessment is practical and on-call-oriented
Identified the subtle backfill-vs-dual-write race condition (stale data overwriting fresh writes) which goes beyond the rubric requirements
Confidence at 55 seems conservatively low given the depth and accuracy of the analysis — the core findings are high-confidence conclusions

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly identified in component_interactions ('Instance 1 reads from user_preferences while Instances 2-3 still read from preferences_json') and in failure_modes ('Rolling deploy in Step 4 creates split-brain reads'). (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 explicitly called out in data_flow_integrity ('No validation step between Step 3 and Step 4') and escalated. (3) Non-resumable backfill identified as a risk with dedicated failure_mode entry ('Backfill job crashes mid-way'). (4) All three specific recommendations present: validation query ('row-count comparison plus spot-check validation query must gate the transition'), feature flag ('Introduce a feature flag to coordinate the read-source switch atomically across all instances'), and resumable backfill ('Add checkpoint/cursor-based resumability. Track last processed user_id'). (5) Verdict is 'NOT READY'.

observations.text: Identified additional risks beyond the rubric: dual-write partial failure, backfill overwriting newer data, rollback gaps between Steps 4-5, and Step 5 rolling deploy creating its own split-brain window
Feature flag recommendation is precisely articulated as 'the single most important missing control' — correct prioritization
Thorough observability review identifying logging, metrics, and tracing gaps that would hamper incident response during migration
Identified the backfill-vs-dual-write race condition (stale overwrite) which is a subtle but real risk not in the rubric
Escalations correctly routed to MJ for architectural fixes rather than recommending a complete migration halt
Database safety section correctly flags Step 6 (drop column) as irreversible and warns against premature execution

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly identified in component_interactions ('During rolling deploy, Instance 1 reads from user_preferences while Instances 2-3 still read from users.preferences_json') and escalated as integration_risk. (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 explicitly flagged as 'the highest-risk gap in the plan' with dedicated escalation. (3) Non-resumable backfill identified in component_interactions ('no progress tracking or resumability') and failure_modes with cursor-based checkpointing recommendation. (4) All three specific recommendations present: validation query (data_flow_integrity section), feature flag for read-source toggle (failure_modes rollback scenario + operational_readiness), resumable backfill with cursor-based pagination (failure_modes). (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth — identified additional risks beyond the rubric: dual-write atomicity, backfill-vs-dual-write race condition (stale data overwrite), malformed JSON handling, and lock contention on large tables
Escalations are well-structured with clear blast radius descriptions and correct routing to MJ for architectural concerns
Observability review is thorough — correctly identifies that on-call would be 'flying blind' with no way to diagnose preference issues during migration
Recommends a practical fallback read pattern (if user_preferences row missing, fall back to preferences_json) which makes the read switch safe regardless of backfill state
Identifies the backfill-vs-dual-write race condition with a concrete fix (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or timestamp comparison) — a subtle issue not in the rubric but operationally critical
Verdict NOT READY is appropriately calibrated — does not overcorrect by halting the migration, instead provides specific fixes to make it ready
